topic: Islam: The Life & Importance of the Prophet Muhammad
subject: religious-studies

items.q: Do Muslims worship the Prophet Muhammad?
items.correct: No - worship in Islam is for Allah alone; Muhammad is revered as the final messenger and the best example to follow, which is why the old label 'Mohammedanism' is rejected.
items.misconception: Muslims worship Muhammad in the way Christians worship Jesus.
items.random_wrong: Muslims worship the cave of Hira where revelation began.
items.why: Equating reverence with worship contradicts Tawhid, the strict oneness of God at the centre of Islam.

items.q: Did Muhammad compose the Qur'an himself?
items.correct: Muslims believe the Qur'an is the word of Allah revealed through the angel Jibril over about twenty-three years; tradition describes Muhammad as unlettered, with scribes recording the revelations.
items.misconception: Muhammad sat down and wrote the Qur'an as his own book.
items.random_wrong: The Qur'an was discovered already written inside the Ka'bah.
items.why: For Muslims the Qur'an's authority rests on divine origin; describing it as Muhammad's composition misrepresents the belief being studied.

items.q: Did Muhammad spread Islam in Makkah by military force?
items.correct: No - for about thirteen years in Makkah the Muslims were a persecuted minority preaching peacefully, and even the later capture of Makkah in 630 was largely bloodless, with a general amnesty.
items.misconception: Islam was spread in Makkah from the start by the sword.
items.random_wrong: Muhammad conquered Makkah before receiving any revelations.
items.why: Students project later conquests backwards; the chronology of persecution before community-building is core syllabus content.

items.q: Which angel do Muslims believe brought the first revelation in the cave of Hira?
items.why: It's Jibril. “Mika'il” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What name did Muhammad earn in Makkah for his honesty?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Al-Amin, 'the trustworthy'. “Al-Faruq” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Who was Muhammad's first wife and earliest supporter?
items.why: Correct answer: Khadijah. “Aisha” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Why is Muhammad called the 'Seal of the Prophets'?
items.correct: Muslims believe he is the final prophet, completing the line of prophets
items.misconception: He sealed his letters with a special ring only
items.random_wrong: He was the first prophet ever sent
items.why: It's Muslims believe he is the final prophet, completing the line of prophets. “He sealed his letters with a special ring only” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year CE do Muslims date the first revelation to Muhammad?
items.why: The real figure is 610 CE. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
